title: Needs Assessment Summary
description: A structured memo to document and communicate your Analysis findings to stakeholders.
content:
NEEDS ASSESSMENT SUMMARY
========================
Project: [Project Name]
Date: [Date]
Prepared by: [Your Name]

BUSINESS PROBLEM
----------------
[Describe the organizational problem or opportunity. What is happening that shouldn't be, or not happening that should be?]

PERFORMANCE GAP
---------------
Current State: [What are people doing or not doing today?]
Desired State: [What should people be doing after training?]
Gap: [Describe the measurable difference]

ROOT CAUSE ANALYSIS
--------------------
Is this a knowledge/skill issue? [Yes / No / Partial]
Explanation: [Describe root cause findings from interviews or observations]

AUDIENCE PROFILE
-----------------
Who: [Role(s) and number of learners]
Experience Level: [Beginner / Intermediate / Advanced]
Tech Access: [Device types, LMS access, bandwidth considerations]
Prior Knowledge: [What relevant knowledge do they already have?]

RECOMMENDED SOLUTION
---------------------
[Training / Job Aid / Process Change / Other — explain why]

PROJECT CONSTRAINTS
--------------------
Timeline: [Required completion date]
Budget: [Approximate range or limit]
Technology: [LMS, authoring tools, delivery format]
SME Availability: [Name, role, weekly hours available]

SUCCESS CRITERIA
-----------------
[How will we know this worked? What metrics will we look at and when?]

NEXT STEPS
----------
[ ] Schedule design kickoff meeting
[ ] Confirm SME availability and meeting cadence
[ ] Draft learning objectives for stakeholder review

Approved by: _________________________  Date: ___________

title: Learner Profile Template
description: Document your target audience in detail before designing anything.
content:
LEARNER PROFILE
===============
Project: [Project Name]
Date: [Date]

AUDIENCE OVERVIEW
------------------
Role(s): [Job title(s) of target learners]
Department(s): [Department or business unit]
Total Number of Learners: [Approximate headcount]
Geography: [Location(s) — remote, on-site, global]

KNOWLEDGE & EXPERIENCE
-----------------------
Time in Role (average): [e.g., 2–5 years]
Prior Training on This Topic: [Yes / No / Some]
Self-Assessed Proficiency: [Novice / Intermediate / Expert]
Key Knowledge Gaps: [List what they don't know that they need to]

ATTITUDES & MOTIVATION
-----------------------
Attitude Toward This Topic: [Resistant / Neutral / Motivated]
Perceived Relevance: [Do they see why this matters? Explain.]
Motivation Drivers: [What do they care about? Career growth, compliance, efficiency?]

LEARNING CONTEXT
-----------------
Where Will They Learn: [At desk / mobile / classroom / mix]
Time Available for Training: [e.g., 30-min max modules, full-day workshop]
Device Access: [Desktop / Laptop / Mobile / Tablet]
LMS or Platform: [Name of system]
Connectivity: [Reliable / Variable / Offline required]

ACCESSIBILITY CONSIDERATIONS
-----------------------------
[List any known accessibility needs or language/literacy considerations]

LEARNER INSIGHTS (from interviews/surveys)
-------------------------------------------
"[Direct quote from learner interview]" — [Role]
"[Direct quote from learner interview]" — [Role]

IMPLICATIONS FOR DESIGN
------------------------
[Based on this profile, what instructional decisions should we make?]

title: Learning Objectives Bank
description: A structured table to draft, review, and map your learning objectives.
content:
LEARNING OBJECTIVES BANK
=========================
Project: [Project Name]
Date: [Date]
Bloom's Level Key: 1=Remember | 2=Understand | 3=Apply | 4=Analyze | 5=Evaluate | 6=Create

TERMINAL OBJECTIVE(S)
----------------------
TO-1: Given [condition], [audience] will be able to [behavior] to [degree/standard].

TO-2: Given [condition], [audience] will be able to [behavior] to [degree/standard].

ENABLING OBJECTIVES
--------------------
[Maps to TO-1]
EO-1.1 | Bloom's Level: [ ] | Objective: [Enabling objective]
EO-1.2 | Bloom's Level: [ ] | Objective: [Enabling objective]
EO-1.3 | Bloom's Level: [ ] | Objective: [Enabling objective]

[Maps to TO-2]
EO-2.1 | Bloom's Level: [ ] | Objective: [Enabling objective]
EO-2.2 | Bloom's Level: [ ] | Objective: [Enabling objective]
EO-2.3 | Bloom's Level: [ ] | Objective: [Enabling objective]

OBJECTIVE QUALITY CHECKLIST
----------------------------
[ ] Each objective uses an observable, measurable verb
[ ] Each objective includes Audience, Behavior, Condition, and Degree
[ ] Bloom's level matches the actual job task complexity
[ ] Objectives are achievable within the course scope
[ ] SME has reviewed and approved all objectives

SME Sign-off: _________________________  Date: ___________
ID Sign-off:  _________________________  Date: ___________

title: Design Document (Blueprint)
description: The core contract between you and your stakeholders before a single asset is built.
content:
DESIGN DOCUMENT
===============
Project: [Project Name]
Version: 1.0
Date: [Date]
Instructional Designer: [Your Name]

PROJECT OVERVIEW
-----------------
Business Problem: [One sentence summary]
Target Audience: [Role, experience level, headcount]
Delivery Format: [e-learning / ILT / VILT / blended / job aid]
Estimated Seat Time: [e.g., 45 minutes]
Platform / LMS: [Platform name]

TERMINAL LEARNING OBJECTIVE
-----------------------------
[Full ABCD objective]

COURSE OUTLINE
--------------
Module 1: [Title]
  - Objective(s): EO-1.1, EO-1.2
  - Content: [Brief description]
  - Instructional Strategy: [e.g., scenario-based, lecture + practice]
  - Assessment: [e.g., 3-question knowledge check]
  - Estimated Time: [x min]

Module 2: [Title]
  - Objective(s): EO-2.1, EO-2.2
  - Content: [Brief description]
  - Instructional Strategy: [e.g., case study, demonstration]
  - Assessment: [e.g., branching scenario]
  - Estimated Time: [x min]

ASSESSMENT STRATEGY
--------------------
Pre-Assessment: [Yes / No] — Purpose: [adaptive skip / baseline data]
Knowledge Checks: [Frequency, format]
Final Assessment: [Format, number of items, passing score]
Performance Task: [If applicable — description]

MEDIA PLAN
-----------
Visuals: [Custom graphics / stock / screenshots]
Audio: [Narration / No narration / Music]
Video: [Talking head / screen capture / animation / none]
Interactivity: [Drag-and-drop / scenarios / simulations / none]

REVIEW AND REVISION PLAN
-------------------------
Alpha Review (internal ID): [Date]
Beta Review (SME): [Date]
Pilot: [Date, audience size]
Full Launch: [Date]

Approved by: _________________________  Date: ___________

title: Storyboard Template
description: A slide-by-slide blueprint for e-learning development. Complete before opening your authoring tool.
content:
STORYBOARD
==========
Project: [Project Name]
Module: [Module Number and Title]
Objective(s): [Objective codes this module addresses]
Version: 1.0 | Date: [Date]

---
SLIDE 001 | Type: Title
---
Visual: [Describe layout and any graphics]
On-Screen Text:
  Title: [Module Title]
  Subtitle: [Optional tagline or duration]
Narration/Audio: [Script or 'No narration']
Animation/Interaction: [Describe if any]
ID Notes: [Any notes for developer]

---
SLIDE 002 | Type: Objective
---
Visual: [e.g., Clean layout with bullet list]
On-Screen Text:
  By the end of this module, you will be able to:
  • [Enabling objective 1]
  • [Enabling objective 2]
Narration/Audio: [Script]
Animation/Interaction: [e.g., None / fade-in bullets]
ID Notes:

---
SLIDE 003 | Type: Content
---
Visual: [Describe graphic, diagram, or image]
On-Screen Text:
  [Headline]
  [Body text — keep to 2–3 key points max]
Narration/Audio: [Script]
Animation/Interaction: [Describe]
ID Notes:

---
SLIDE 004 | Type: Knowledge Check
---
Visual: [Question layout]
Question: [Question text]
A. [Option A]
B. [Option B]
C. [Option C]
D. [Option D]
Correct Answer: [Letter]
Feedback (Correct): [Reinforcement text]
Feedback (Incorrect): [Corrective explanation]
Narration/Audio: [If any]
ID Notes:

[Repeat slide blocks as needed]

title: Launch Communication Email
description: A ready-to-send email template for notifying learners that training is live.
content:
SUBJECT: New Training Available: [Course Title]

Hi [First Name / Team],

I'm pleased to let you know that [Course Title] is now available for you to complete.

WHY THIS TRAINING MATTERS
--------------------------
[Explain in 2–3 plain-language sentences why this training exists and what problem it solves. Connect it to something learners care about — their job, their team, a business goal.]

WHAT YOU'LL LEARN
------------------
By completing this training, you will be able to:
• [Key skill or outcome 1]
• [Key skill or outcome 2]
• [Key skill or outcome 3]

THE DETAILS
-----------
Format: [e-learning / live session / workshop]
Estimated Time: [X minutes]
Deadline: [Date, or 'Complete at your own pace']
Access: [Link to LMS or calendar invite]

HOW TO GET STARTED
-------------------
1. Click here: [LMS link]
2. Log in with your [company] credentials
3. Find [Course Title] in [location in LMS]

NEED HELP?
----------
If you have trouble accessing the training, contact [support email or name].
For questions about the content, reach out to [SME or manager name].

Thank you for your time and commitment to [learning goal].

[Your Name]
[Title]
[Department]

title: Manager Briefing One-Pager
description: Equip managers to support and reinforce training before and after launch.
content:
MANAGER BRIEFING: [Course Title]
=================================
Prepared for: [Manager Name / Management Team]
Date: [Date]

WHAT'S HAPPENING
-----------------
[Course Title] is launching on [date] for all [role/team name].
Estimated time: [X minutes] | Deadline: [Date]
Access: [LMS link or location]

WHY THIS MATTERS
-----------------
[Explain the business problem this training addresses. Be specific — what will change for the team as a result?]

YOUR ROLE AS A MANAGER
-----------------------
BEFORE the training:
[ ] Let your team know this is coming and why it's important
[ ] Block time on their calendars if needed
[ ] Set the expectation that completion by [deadline] is required/encouraged

DURING the training:
[ ] Be available to answer questions about how the content applies to your team's work

AFTER the training:
[ ] Have a brief team conversation about key takeaways
[ ] Connect training concepts to real on-the-job situations
[ ] Recognize and reinforce when team members apply what they learned

FAQ FOR MANAGERS
-----------------
Q: What if someone can't complete by the deadline?
A: [Instructions for extensions or exceptions]

Q: Who do I contact with questions about the content?
A: [SME name and contact]

Q: How will I know who on my team has completed it?
A: [LMS reporting instructions]

CONTACT
--------
For questions about this training initiative, contact:
[Instructional Designer Name] | [Email]

title: Level 1 Reaction Survey
description: A concise post-training survey to collect learner reaction data.
content:
POST-TRAINING SURVEY: [Course Title]
=====================================
Thank you for completing [Course Title]. This short survey (3–5 minutes) helps us improve future training.
All responses are anonymous.

SECTION 1: RELEVANCE
---------------------
1. The content of this training was relevant to my job.
   [ ] Strongly Agree  [ ] Agree  [ ] Neutral  [ ] Disagree  [ ] Strongly Disagree

2. The training addressed a real challenge I face in my work.
   [ ] Strongly Agree  [ ] Agree  [ ] Neutral  [ ] Disagree  [ ] Strongly Disagree

SECTION 2: QUALITY
-------------------
3. The training was well-organized and easy to follow.
   [ ] Strongly Agree  [ ] Agree  [ ] Neutral  [ ] Disagree  [ ] Strongly Disagree

4. The activities and examples helped me understand the content.
   [ ] Strongly Agree  [ ] Agree  [ ] Neutral  [ ] Disagree  [ ] Strongly Disagree

5. The length of the training was appropriate for the content.
   [ ] Too Short  [ ] About Right  [ ] Too Long

SECTION 3: INTENT TO APPLY
----------------------------
6. I plan to apply what I learned in this training to my work.
   [ ] Definitely  [ ] Probably  [ ] Unsure  [ ] Probably Not  [ ] Definitely Not

7. What is the most useful thing you will take away from this training?
   [Open text field]

8. What would make this training more effective?
   [Open text field]

OVERALL RATING
--------------
9. Overall, I would rate this training:
   [ ] Excellent  [ ] Good  [ ] Fair  [ ] Poor

Thank you for your feedback!

title: SME Interview Guide
description: A structured question guide for extracting content, task knowledge, and performance insights from subject matter experts.
content:
SME INTERVIEW GUIDE
===================
Project: [Project Name]
Interview Date: [Date]
Interviewer: [Your Name]
SME Name: [SME Name]
SME Title/Role: [Title]
Department: [Department]

PRE-INTERVIEW PREPARATION
--------------------------
[ ] Review any existing SOPs, job aids, or documentation related to the topic
[ ] Draft your key questions in advance — do not read them verbatim
[ ] Confirm session length: [e.g., 60 minutes]
[ ] Send a brief agenda to the SME 24–48 hours in advance
[ ] Have this guide open for note-taking during the call

OPENING (5 minutes)
--------------------
Introduce yourself and set context:
"We're designing a training program on [topic]. I want to understand the job from your perspective — what people need to know and do, and where performance currently falls short. There are no wrong answers; you're the expert."

BACKGROUND QUESTIONS
---------------------
1. How long have you been in this role, and what does a typical day look like?
   Notes: _______________________________________________

2. Who else performs this job, and how much variation is there across individuals or locations?
   Notes: _______________________________________________

PERFORMANCE GAP QUESTIONS
--------------------------
3. What does a top performer do differently from someone who struggles?
   Notes: _______________________________________________

4. What mistakes do people most commonly make? What are the consequences?
   Notes: _______________________________________________

5. When did you last see someone do this poorly? What happened?
   Notes: _______________________________________________

TASK AND CONTENT QUESTIONS
---------------------------
6. Walk me through exactly how you would do [specific task] — step by step.
   Notes: _______________________________________________

7. What does someone need to know before they can do this correctly?
   Notes: _______________________________________________

8. Where are the key decision points — where does someone have to choose what to do next?
   Notes: _______________________________________________

9. Are there rules, regulations, or standards that govern how this is done?
   Notes: _______________________________________________

10. What resources (job aids, SOPs, tools) do people currently use, or should be using?
    Notes: _______________________________________________

TRAINING DESIGN QUESTIONS
--------------------------
11. What is the single most important thing a new person needs to learn first?
    Notes: _______________________________________________

12. What is the biggest misconception people have about this topic?
    Notes: _______________________________________________

13. Can you describe a realistic scenario that would make a good practice activity?
    Notes: _______________________________________________

CLOSING (5 minutes)
--------------------
"Is there anything important I haven't asked about?"
Notes: _______________________________________________

"Who else should I talk to — other SMEs, or people who have recently learned this skill?"
Follow-up contacts: _______________________________________________

POST-INTERVIEW NOTES
---------------------
Key takeaways:
1. _______________________________________________
2. _______________________________________________
3. _______________________________________________

Content gaps to resolve: _______________________________________________
Follow-up questions: _______________________________________________
Documents requested: _______________________________________________
Next contact date: _______________________________________________

title: Project Scope Statement
description: Define project boundaries, deliverables, constraints, and stakeholder sign-off before design begins.
content:
PROJECT SCOPE STATEMENT
========================
Project Name: [Project Name]
Prepared by: [Your Name]
Date: [Date]
Version: 1.0
Status: [ ] Draft  [ ] Under Review  [ ] Approved

PROJECT OVERVIEW
-----------------
Business Need: [Why does this project exist? What problem does it solve?]
Sponsor: [Executive or business owner name and title]
Primary Stakeholders: [List names and roles]
Target Audience: [Role(s), location(s), estimated headcount]
Delivery Format: [e-learning / ILT / VILT / blended / job aid / other]
Authoring Tool: [Articulate Rise / Storyline / PowerPoint / other]
LMS / Platform: [Platform name]

IN-SCOPE DELIVERABLES
----------------------
[ ] Needs Assessment Summary
[ ] Learner Profile
[ ] Learning Objectives
[ ] Design Document
[ ] [X] modules of [Y format] — estimated [Z] minutes total seat time
[ ] Assessments: [List types — pre-test, knowledge checks, final assessment]
[ ] [Additional deliverables, e.g., job aid, facilitator guide, manager brief]
[ ] Evaluation plan through Kirkpatrick Level [1 / 2 / 3]

OUT OF SCOPE
------------
The following are explicitly NOT included in this project:
• [e.g., Translation or localization to other languages]
• [e.g., Custom LMS configuration or integrations]
• [e.g., Video production with actors or studio recording]
• [e.g., Ongoing content maintenance after launch]

ASSUMPTIONS
-----------
• SME availability: [X hours per week / named SMEs confirmed]
• Stakeholder review turnaround: [X business days per review cycle]
• Client will provide all source content, SOPs, and reference documents
• [Additional assumptions]

CONSTRAINTS
-----------
• Timeline: Must launch by [date]
• Budget: [Amount or 'To be confirmed']
• Technology: [Any platform, accessibility, or compatibility requirements]
• [Additional constraints]

PROJECT MILESTONES
------------------
Milestone                         | Target Date    | Owner
----------------------------------|----------------|--------
Analysis complete                 | [Date]         | [Name]
Design Document approved          | [Date]         | [Name]
Alpha (internal review)           | [Date]         | [Name]
Beta (SME/client review)          | [Date]         | [Name]
Pilot session                     | [Date]         | [Name]
Full launch                       | [Date]         | [Name]

CHANGE CONTROL
--------------
Any changes to scope, timeline, or budget after sign-off require a formal change request and re-approval by the project sponsor.

SIGN-OFF
--------
By signing below, stakeholders confirm this scope statement accurately represents the agreed project boundaries.

Project Sponsor:       _________________________  Date: ___________
Instructional Designer: _________________________  Date: ___________
Stakeholder:           _________________________  Date: ___________

title: Gap Analysis Worksheet
description: Systematically compare current vs. desired performance to identify what training can and cannot fix.
content:
GAP ANALYSIS WORKSHEET
=======================
Project: [Project Name]
Prepared by: [Your Name]
Date: [Date]
Data Sources: [ ] Interviews  [ ] Surveys  [ ] Observation  [ ] Performance data  [ ] Documentation review

HOW TO USE THIS WORKSHEET
--------------------------
For each performance area, document the current state, desired state, and the resulting gap. Identify the root cause and recommend a solution. Use this to justify your training scope and design decisions.

--- PERFORMANCE AREA 1: [Name of task or competency] ---

Current State (what is actually happening):
[Describe observed or reported behaviors, error rates, or outcomes]

Desired State (what should be happening):
[Describe the standard, benchmark, or expected behavior/outcome]

The Gap:
[Describe the measurable difference between current and desired state]

Root Cause Analysis:
[ ] Lack of knowledge — people don't know what to do
[ ] Lack of skill — people know but can't do it consistently
[ ] Lack of motivation — people know and can, but choose not to
[ ] Environmental/system barrier — tools, process, or resources are in the way
Notes: [Explain root cause evidence]

Recommended Solution:
[ ] Training  [ ] Job Aid  [ ] Process Change  [ ] Coaching/Management  [ ] System Fix  [ ] Multiple
Rationale: [Why this solution addresses the root cause]

Priority: [ ] Critical  [ ] High  [ ] Medium  [ ] Low
Impact if unaddressed: [Brief description]

--- PERFORMANCE AREA 2: [Name of task or competency] ---

Current State: _______________________________________________
Desired State: _______________________________________________
The Gap: _______________________________________________
Root Cause: [ ] Knowledge  [ ] Skill  [ ] Motivation  [ ] Environment
Notes: _______________________________________________
Recommended Solution: [ ] Training  [ ] Job Aid  [ ] Process  [ ] Other
Priority: [ ] Critical  [ ] High  [ ] Medium  [ ] Low

--- PERFORMANCE AREA 3: [Name of task or competency] ---

Current State: _______________________________________________
Desired State: _______________________________________________
The Gap: _______________________________________________
Root Cause: [ ] Knowledge  [ ] Skill  [ ] Motivation  [ ] Environment
Notes: _______________________________________________
Recommended Solution: [ ] Training  [ ] Job Aid  [ ] Process  [ ] Other
Priority: [ ] Critical  [ ] High  [ ] Medium  [ ] Low

SUMMARY TABLE
-------------
Performance Area     | Gap Severity | Root Cause   | Solution     | Priority
---------------------|--------------|--------------|--------------|----------
[Area 1]             | High/Med/Low | K/S/M/E      | Training/etc | Critical
[Area 2]             | High/Med/Low | K/S/M/E      | Training/etc | High
[Area 3]             | High/Med/Low | K/S/M/E      | Training/etc | Medium

TRAINING SCOPE RECOMMENDATION
------------------------------
Based on this analysis, the following gaps should be addressed through formal training:
• [Gap 1 — brief justification]
• [Gap 2 — brief justification]

The following gaps should be addressed through non-training interventions:
• [Gap X — recommended intervention and owner]

Reviewed by: _________________________  Date: ___________

title: Evaluation Plan
description: A structured plan for measuring training effectiveness across all four Kirkpatrick levels — built before launch, not after.
content:
EVALUATION PLAN
================
Course / Program: [Course Title]
Prepared by: [Your Name]
Date: [Date]
Evaluation Framework: Kirkpatrick Four Levels

PURPOSE
--------
This plan documents how we will measure the effectiveness of [Course Title], who is responsible for each data collection activity, and when results will be reported.

LEVEL 1 — REACTION (Did learners find it valuable?)
----------------------------------------------------
What we'll measure:
• Relevance of content to job role
• Quality and clarity of instruction
• Learner intent to apply

Method: Post-training survey (5–8 questions)
Tool: [Survey platform — e.g., LMS built-in, Microsoft Forms, SurveyMonkey]
Timing: Immediately after course completion
Who collects: [Name / Role]
Target response rate: [e.g., 80%]
Success benchmark: [e.g., ≥4.0/5.0 average on relevance and quality items]
Report to: [Stakeholder name]

LEVEL 2 — LEARNING (Did they acquire the intended knowledge and skill?)
-----------------------------------------------------------------------
What we'll measure:
• Knowledge of [key concepts]
• Ability to [perform or identify target behavior]

Method: [Pre/post assessment / final quiz / performance demonstration]
Format: [Multiple choice / scenario / practical task]
Passing score: [e.g., 80%]
Where assessed: [In-course / LMS / separate assessment platform]
Who reviews results: [Name / Role]
Success benchmark: [e.g., ≥80% of learners pass on first attempt]
Report to: [Stakeholder name]

LEVEL 3 — BEHAVIOR (Are they applying it on the job?)
------------------------------------------------------
What we'll measure:
• [Specific observable behavior change, e.g., 'Uses the new intake process for all new cases']
• [Behavior change 2]

Methods (select all that apply):
[ ] Manager observation checklist
[ ] 30/60/90-day follow-up learner survey
[ ] Performance data review
[ ] Supervisor rating or calibration session

Timing: [e.g., 30–60 days post-training completion]
Who collects: [Name / Role — often manager or HR partner]
Success benchmark: [e.g., ≥70% of learners report applying at least one skill weekly]
Report to: [Stakeholder name]
Known limitations: [e.g., difficult to isolate training as sole cause; manager participation may vary]

LEVEL 4 — RESULTS (Did it affect the business?)
-------------------------------------------------
Business metrics we are tracking:
• [Metric 1, e.g., Error rate on X process] — Current baseline: [X%]
• [Metric 2, e.g., Customer complaint volume] — Current baseline: [X/month]
• [Metric 3, e.g., Time to competency for new hires] — Current baseline: [X weeks]

Data source: [e.g., Operations dashboard, HR system, QA reports]
Measurement window: [e.g., 6 months post-launch]
Who owns this data: [Name / Department]
Reporting cadence: [Monthly / Quarterly]
Success benchmark: [e.g., 15% reduction in error rate within 6 months of launch]

EVALUATION TIMELINE
-------------------
Action                                  | When               | Owner
----------------------------------------|--------------------|----------
Level 1 survey goes live                | At course launch   | [Name]
Level 2 results reviewed                | Week 2 post-launch | [Name]
Level 3 follow-up survey sent           | Day 30 post-launch | [Name]
Level 3 data analyzed and reported      | Day 45 post-launch | [Name]
Level 4 baseline confirmed (pre-launch) | [Date]             | [Name]
Level 4 interim review                  | [Date]             | [Name]
Final evaluation report to stakeholders | [Date]             | [Name]

REPORTING FORMAT
----------------
Results will be reported to [stakeholder audience] via [e.g., PowerPoint deck, email summary, dashboard] on [frequency].

Approved by: _________________________  Date: ___________

title: VILT Facilitation Guide
description: A complete run-of-show guide for facilitating a virtual instructor-led training session, including tech setup, agenda, and troubleshooting.
content:
VILT FACILITATION GUIDE
========================
Session Title: [Course / Session Title]
Duration: [X hours / X minutes]
Platform: [Zoom / Teams / Webex / other]
Max Participants: [Number]
Facilitator: [Primary Facilitator Name]
Producer / Co-facilitator: [Name — manages chat, tech, breakout rooms]
Date(s): [Session date(s)]

PRE-SESSION SETUP (Complete 30–60 minutes before start time)
-------------------------------------------------------------
Technology
[ ] Log in to [platform] and test audio, video, and screen share
[ ] Load slides and have them ready — do not share screen yet
[ ] Test polling tool: [Mentimeter / Slido / platform built-in / none]
[ ] Pre-configure breakout rooms if used (name them, assign participants if known)
[ ] Enable waiting room / lobby
[ ] Disable participant screen share unless the activity requires it
[ ] Confirm recording settings (record or not, and where it saves)
[ ] Have the backup slide deck link ready to paste in chat

Materials
[ ] Participant workbook or job aid link ready to paste in chat
[ ] Welcome message written and ready to copy-paste
[ ] Producer briefed on their responsibilities and timing cues

WELCOME MESSAGE (Paste in chat as participants join)
-----------------------------------------------------
Welcome to [Session Title]!
• Session runs [start time] – [end time] [timezone]
• Materials: [link, if applicable]
• Please mute your microphone unless speaking
• Use chat to ask questions at any time — [Producer name] is monitoring
• We'll take a break at approximately [time]

SESSION AGENDA
--------------
TIME        | SEGMENT                    | TYPE                | FACILITATOR NOTES
------------|----------------------------|---------------------|-----------------------------------
[00:00]     | Welcome & logistics        | Facilitator talk    | Quick audio check — unmute all briefly
[00:05]     | Icebreaker / opening poll  | Participant activity | [Poll question or chat prompt text]
[00:10]     | Objectives & agenda        | Facilitator talk    | Show objectives slide
[00:15]     | [Content block 1]          | [Lecture/Demo/etc]  | [Key talking points]
[00:35]     | Activity or breakout       | Participant activity| [Instructions — see verbatim script below]
[00:50]     | Debrief                    | Discussion          | [Debrief questions]
[01:00]     | BREAK                      | —                   | 10 min; return by [time]
[01:10]     | [Content block 2]          | [Type]              | [Key talking points]
[01:30]     | Scenario / case study      | Participant activity| [Instructions]
[01:45]     | Q&A                        | Discussion          | Use parking lot for unanswered questions
[01:55]     | Action planning            | Participant activity| Prompt: 'What's one thing you'll do differently?'
[02:00]     | Close & survey link        | Facilitator talk    | Paste Level 1 survey link in chat

ACTIVITY SCRIPTS (Read these verbatim to ensure clarity)
---------------------------------------------------------
ICEBREAKER:
"In the chat, type [e.g., your role and one word that describes your current experience with this topic]. I'll give you 30 seconds — go ahead."

BREAKOUT ROOMS:
"I'm going to send you into breakout rooms of [X] people. You'll have [Y] minutes to [task]. Designate one person as the note-taker. I'll send a 2-minute warning before closing the rooms. Any questions before we start?"

DEBRIEF:
"Welcome back. Who wants to share what their group discussed? [Pause.] What surprised you? What would make this hardest to apply in your role?"

ACTION PLANNING:
"Before we close, take 60 seconds and type in the chat: one specific thing you will do differently in the next two weeks based on today. I'll give you a moment."

TROUBLESHOOTING
----------------
Problem                              | Solution
-------------------------------------|------------------------------------------
Participant can't hear audio         | Ask them to leave and rejoin; check audio settings
Screen share freezes                 | Stop share, wait 10 seconds, restart
Participant with disruptive background| Privately mute via host controls; send direct message
Breakout rooms not working           | Use chat groups instead; post instructions in chat
Facilitator loses connection         | Producer takes over; facilitator rejoins within 5 min
Poll tool fails                      | Ask participants to respond in chat instead

POST-SESSION CHECKLIST
-----------------------
[ ] Paste Level 1 survey link in chat before closing
[ ] Thank participants and formally close the session
[ ] Save or download the chat log
[ ] Send follow-up email: recording link (if applicable), materials, action item reminder
[ ] Complete debrief notes below

FACILITATOR DEBRIEF NOTES
--------------------------
What went well: _______________________________________________
What to improve for next time: _______________________________________________
Participant questions to follow up on: _______________________________________________
Attendance: [X] registered / [X] attended
Survey response rate: _________________  Average rating: _______________

title: SME Review Tracker
description: Track, manage, and resolve SME feedback across review cycles — with a formal sign-off record.
content:
SME REVIEW TRACKER
==================
Project: [Project Name]
Module / Asset: [Module Name or Asset Description]
Review Cycle: [ ] Alpha  [ ] Beta  [ ] Final  [ ] Other: ___
Materials sent to SME: [Date]
Review deadline: [Date]
SME Name(s): [Name(s)]

HOW TO USE
-----------
1. Send this tracker to SMEs along with the content under review
2. SMEs log each comment as a separate row in the Feedback Log
3. ID/developer reviews each comment and records their decision
4. SME confirms all resolutions before signing off

FEEDBACK LOG
------------
#   | Location (Slide/Section) | SME Comment                         | Priority          | ID Decision            | Status     | Notes
----|--------------------------|-------------------------------------|-------------------|------------------------|------------|------
1   | [Slide # or section]     | [Exact feedback]                    | [ ] Must  [ ] Nice | Accept / Modify / Reject| [ ] Open   |
2   |                          |                                     |                   |                        | [ ] Open   |
3   |                          |                                     |                   |                        | [ ] Open   |
4   |                          |                                     |                   |                        | [ ] Open   |
5   |                          |                                     |                   |                        | [ ] Open   |
6   |                          |                                     |                   |                        | [ ] Open   |
7   |                          |                                     |                   |                        | [ ] Open   |
8   |                          |                                     |                   |                        | [ ] Open   |
9   |                          |                                     |                   |                        | [ ] Open   |
10  |                          |                                     |                   |                        | [ ] Open   |

DECISION DEFINITIONS
--------------------
Accept  — Change will be made exactly as requested
Modify  — Change will be made, but adapted (explain in Notes)
Reject  — Change will NOT be made (ID must document rationale below)
Defer   — Valid point, out of scope for this version; logged for future revision

RESOLUTION SUMMARY
-------------------
Total comments received:        ______
Accepted (no modification):     ______
Modified (with adaptation):     ______
Rejected (with rationale):      ______
Deferred to future version:     ______
Open / Pending:                 ______

REJECTION RATIONALE (Required for any rejected item)
------------------------------------------------------
Item # ___: [Explain why this change was not made and what was done instead]
Item # ___: [Explain why this change was not made and what was done instead]

REVIEW CYCLE SIGN-OFF
----------------------
By signing below, the SME confirms that all open items have been resolved to their satisfaction and the content is approved to proceed to the next stage.

SME Name:  _________________________  Signature: _________________________  Date: ___________
ID Name:   _________________________  Signature: _________________________  Date: ___________

REVISION HISTORY
----------------
Version | Date    | Changed by | Summary
--------|---------|------------|----------------------------------
1.0     | [Date]  | [Name]     | Initial draft sent for SME review
1.1     | [Date]  | [Name]     | Revisions from [Alpha/Beta] feedback
